recommendations.riskRationale: CDK bootstrap role — must never be modified or consolidated. It is managed by the CDK toolkit and will be overwritten or broken on next 'cdk bootstrap' run.
recommendations.rationale: This role matches the CDK bootstrap naming pattern (cdk-hnb659fds-*) and is explicitly excluded from consolidation per policy. It trusts cloudformation.amazonaws.com and was recently active (2026-04-03). Any modification will cause CDK deployments to fail.

recommendations.riskRationale: AWS-reserved SSO role managed entirely by AWS IAM Identity Center. Manual modification or deletion will break SSO login for all assigned users in this account.
recommendations.rationale: This role matches the AWSReservedSSO_* naming pattern and is explicitly excluded from consolidation per policy. It trusts a SAML federation provider managed by AWS SSO and was last used in 2024. It cannot be safely modified outside of IAM Identity Center.

recommendations.riskRationale: AdministratorAccess granted to ec2, lambda, and ssm service principals simultaneously is an extremely broad attack surface. Any compromised EC2 instance, Lambda function, or SSM-managed host can escalate to full account admin.
recommendations.rationale: This role has never been used (lastUsed: null) and was created in August 2020 — over 5 years ago. The multi-service trust policy (ec2, lambda, ssm) with AdministratorAccess is a critical security finding. Recommend investigating whether this role is still needed at all. If required, replace AdministratorAccess with least-privilege policies scoped to each use case, or split into separate roles per service principal. Do not consolidate into any other role.

recommendations.riskRationale: Grants AdministratorAccess to the root of an external AWS account (075587720175) with no conditions. Any principal in that external account can assume full admin over this account.
recommendations.rationale: This role has never been used (lastUsed: null) and was created in August 2020. Cross-account admin trust with no MFA or condition constraints is a severe security posture issue. Verify whether account 075587720175 is still a trusted partner or a managed services vendor. If no longer needed, this role should be deleted. If still required, add at minimum an aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent or ExternalId condition. Cannot be safely consolidated with any other role due to its unique cross-account trust.

recommendations.riskRationale: AdministratorAccess assumable by any principal within the AWS Organization (o-8p9ny5y26a) from this account's root — a very broad intra-org trust. Never used since creation.
recommendations.rationale: Created 2026-04-03 and never used. The PrincipalOrgID condition scopes trust to the organization, which is a positive control, but allowing the account root plus any org member to assume a full-admin role with no further condition (no role path, no specific principal ARN, no MFA requirement) is overly permissive. Recommend scoping the Principal to specific roles or users rather than the account root, and adding an MFA condition. Cannot be consolidated with other roles due to its unique org-scoped trust policy.

recommendations.riskRationale: An IAM Group with AdministratorAccess attached is a standing privilege escalation path for any user added to this group.
recommendations.rationale: The 'wheel' group grants AdministratorAccess to all its members as a persistent standing permission — not a time-bound or role-assumption-based grant. Best practice is to remove standing AdministratorAccess from groups and replace with a permission to assume a time-limited admin role (e.g., RescorOrganizationAdministrator with MFA). Groups are a different entity type from roles and cannot be technically merged with roles; however, the membership and permission design should be reviewed alongside the admin roles. No consolidation is technically possible.

analysis.summary: All 6 entities share only AdministratorAccess and must be kept separate due to mutually exclusive trust principals, CDK/SSO management constraints, and entity-type differences — however, MasterSetup_Role (unused 5+ years, multi-service admin trust) and RescorCloudAdministrator (unused 5+ years, unconditioned cross-account admin) are strong candidates for deletion, and the remaining admin roles all warrant security hardening before any further review.

recommendations.riskRationale: Both roles have identical managed policies (AGTest-CloudWatchLogs, AGTest-EC2Describe, AGTest-S3ReadOnly), identical ec2.amazonaws.com trust principals, identical tags, and neither has been used since creation. Merging eliminates a redundant role with zero net permission change.
recommendations.rationale: AGTest-AppRole1 and AGTest-AppRole2 are exact duplicates: same three managed policies, same trust policy (ec2.amazonaws.com), same Application/Environment tags (AccessGuard/Test), no CloudFormation stack tags indicating separate automation ownership, and both have null lastUsed with creation dates seconds apart. No permission expansion occurs. AGTest-AppRole2 is retained arbitrarily (both are equivalent); either could serve as the target. The AutoCleanup=True tag suggests these are ephemeral test resources, reinforcing that the duplicate was likely an accident.

recommendations.riskRationale: AGTest-EC2Describe is a read-only describe policy with no write or sensitive service access. The only risk is over-privilege: DataRole1 consumers would gain EC2 describe permissions they currently lack, but this is a low-sensitivity expansion.
recommendations.rationale: AGTest-DataRole1 and AGTest-DataRole2 share 3 of 4 policies (75% overlap: AGTest-CloudWatchLogs, AGTest-RDSReadOnly, AGTest-S3ReadOnly). The sole difference is that DataRole2 also holds AGTest-EC2Describe. Both have identical ec2.amazonaws.com trust policies, identical AccessGuard/Test tags, no CFN stack ownership tags, and null lastUsed. Consolidation into DataRole2 is viable but warrants a brief review to confirm that any workloads previously assigned DataRole1 are intentionally being granted EC2 describe access. Because EC2Describe is read-only and non-sensitive, this is a low-risk addition if confirmed acceptable by the application owner.

recommendations.riskRationale: AGTest-RDSReadOnly is a read-only policy. However, merging the App and Data role families into a single role crosses a functional boundary — EC2 app instances would gain RDS read access they were deliberately denied — which may violate least-privilege intent even if the technical risk is low.
recommendations.rationale: After consolidating the AppRole pair and DataRole pair separately, the resulting two roles (one App, one Data) share 2 of 4 policies (50% overlap), falling below the 70% threshold for automatic grouping. A further merge into a single role is architecturally possible but not recommended without explicit confirmation: the App and Data role families appear intentionally segmented (EC2-only vs. EC2+RDS access patterns). Retain as two distinct roles unless the application team confirms a single combined role is acceptable.

analysis.summary: The four roles decompose into two exact duplicates (AppRole1/AppRole2, safe to consolidate immediately) and two near-duplicates (DataRole1/DataRole2, safe to consolidate after confirming the EC2Describe addition is acceptable); further merging across the App and Data families is not recommended as it would cross intentional functional privilege boundaries.
